column5: input, performance, credential, control
column6:
1st: @output, seeing a single number might lead me to think I might be the 1 out of 10 chances of AI getting it wrong, but looking at the narrow range with high certainty (95%) lead me to think AI is quite sure, and I might not escape the chance/low likelihood.
2nd: @performance, the mean error range +- 15% that’s ok, but max error of +-30 is too much, more than 60% range, tends to let me think it’s more like random guess, which lower the trust for me.
3rd @te and @prior let me to see similar people like mine, give me some gut feeling to trust the AI, whereas 1st and 2nd let me trust AI based on rational thinking, @te and @prior let me trust kinda emotionally.
@se and @te provide intuitive gut feeling for me the same people like me, provide a reference to show their profile (more context, compare mine with others).
